paper_title: Single Cell Analysis of Variable Chlorophyll Fluorescence Methods

methods.method_name: Single Cell Variable Chlorophyll Fluorescence Analysis
methods.description: This method uses a pulse-amplitude-modulated (PAM) chlorophyll fluorescence imaging system to estimate the photosynthetic capacity of individual cells, specifically comparing cells with even versus altered chloroplast distributions. It involves measuring effective PSII quantum yield (YII), determining the wavelength dependent functional absorption cross-section of PSII (Sigma(II)λ), deriving ETR(II) curve fit parameters, and performing statistical analysis.

steps.description: Singlecell variable chlorophyll fluorescence was investigated with a red-green-blue (RGB) pulse-amplitude-modulated (PAM) chlorophyll fluorescence imaging system (RGB Microscopy-IPAM, Heinz Walz GmbH, Germany) mounted on an epifluorescence microscope (model Axiostar plus FL, Carl Zeiss, Jena, Germany). The setup is described in detail by Trampe et al. (2011). Measurements were performed with a 20x objective (Plan-Apochromate, Carl Zeiss GmbH, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: setup PAM fluorescence microscopy system for single cell analysis
inputs: RGB PAM chlorophyll fluorescence imaging system
outputs: Configured microscopy and fluorescence imaging system

steps.description: Non-actinic blue measuring light was used
steps.searchable_summary: apply non-actinic blue measuring light for PAM fluorescence

steps.description: the effective PSII quantum yield (YII) was determined with a high intensity saturating BL pulse.
steps.searchable_summary: apply high intensity saturating pulse for PAM fluorescence

steps.description: The maximum fluorescence yield (F m ) correction factor was set to 1.03, to account for signal losses due to LED heating.
steps.searchable_summary: apply correction factor to maximum fluorescence yield (Fm)

steps.description: The Y(II) values were calculated using the system software (ImagingWin, Heinz Walz GmbH, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: calculate effective PSII quantum yield (YII) from fluorescence data
inputs: Raw/corrected fluorescence data (e.g., Fm, Fs, Fv)
outputs: Calculated effective PSII quantum yield (YII) values

steps.description: For each sample, quantitative analysis of variable chlorophyll fluorescence was performed on 10 cells with evenly distributed chloroplasts and 10 cells with an altered chloroplast distribution. Only up to 5 cells with an altered chloroplast distribution were analyzed in the control group because of lower occurrence.
steps.searchable_summary: select single cells based on chloroplast distribution for quantitative analysis
outputs: Identified and categorized single cells for analysis

steps.description: For each sample, quantitative analysis of variable chlorophyll fluorescence was performed on 10 cells with evenly distributed chloroplasts and 10 cells with an altered chloroplast distribution. Only up to 5 cells with an altered chloroplast distribution were analyzed in the control group because of lower occurrence.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quantitative analysis of fluorescence data on selected single cells
inputs: Calculated YII values for selected cells
outputs: Quantitative YII dataset per cell type per sample

steps.description: The wavelength dependent functional absorption cross-section of photosystem (PS) II [Sigma(II)λ ] was determined with a multicolor chlorophyll fluorescence analyzer.
steps.searchable_summary: determine wavelength dependent functional absorption cross-section of PSII (Sigma(II)λ)

steps.description: ETR(II) curve fit parameter were derived from ETR light curves using the equation of Eilers and Peeters (1988).
steps.searchable_summary: derive ETR(II) curve fit parameters from ETR light curves using Eilers and Peeters equation

steps.description: Sigma(II)λ data were used to account for differences in the light absorbed by PS(II).
steps.searchable_summary: adjust fluorescence data based on PSII light absorption differences using Sigma(II)λ
outputs: Fluorescence data adjusted for light absorption

steps.description: Using the fold change to a WL source [Sigma(II)λ fold WL] similar light intensities were adjusted during the experiments.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust experimental light intensities based on Sigma(II)λ fold change
inputs: Sigma(II)λ fold change relative to WL source
outputs: Adjusted light intensity settings for experiments

steps.description: Statistically significant differences between the treatments are indicated as different small letters (p < 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: perform statistical analysis to compare quantitative data between groups
inputs: Quantitative data (e.g., YII, ETR parameters) for different treatments/cell types
outputs: Statistical significance results (e.g., p-values, group letters)
